fairy.en: a small magical being in stories, often with wings

ex.en: The fairy granted her three wishes.
ex.ko: 요정은 그녀에게 세 가지 소원을 들어주었다.

ex.en: His answer to the question was false.
ex.ko: 그 질문에 대한 그의 대답은 틀렸다.

false.en: not real or genuine; made to look like the real thing

ex.en: She wore false eyelashes to the party.
ex.ko: 그녀는 파티에 인조 속눈썹을 붙이고 갔다.

family.en: a group of people related to each other, such as parents and children

ex.en: We had dinner with my family last night.
She became famous after winning the competition.

fan.en: someone who likes and admires a person, team, or activity very much

ex.en: He is a huge fan of that soccer team.

fan.en: a machine with blades that turns to create a current of air

ex.en: Turn on the fan, it's too hot in here.
ex.ko: 선풍기 좀 켜, 여기 너무 더워.

farm.en: an area of land used to grow crops or raise animals

ex.en: My grandparents live on a farm.

farm.en: grow crops or raise animals on land

ex.en: His family has farmed this land for generations.
ex.ko: 그의 가족은 대대로 이 땅에서 농사를 지어왔다.

farmer.en: a person who owns or works on a farm

ex.en: The farmer got up early to feed the animals.
ex.ko: 그 농부는 동물들에게 먹이를 주려고 일찍 일어났다.

fast.en: moving or able to move quickly

ex.en: He drives a very fast car.
She ran fast to catch the bus.

fast.en: eat no food for a period of time

ex.en: Some people fast during religious holidays.
ex.ko: 어떤 사람들은 종교 명절 동안 금식한다.

fat.en: having a lot of extra flesh on the body

ex.en: The cat got fat from eating too much.
ex.ko: 그 고양이는 너무 많이 먹어서 뚱뚱해졌다.

fat.en: the greasy substance found in food and in the body

ex.en: This meat has too much fat.
My father works at a hospital.
Pizza is my favorite food.

favorite.en: the person or thing that someone likes best

ex.en: This song is my favorite from the album.
ex.ko: 이 노래가 그 앨범에서 내가 가장 좋아하는 곡이다.

february.en: the second month of the year

ex.en: Her birthday is in February.

feed.en: give food to a person or animal

ex.en: She feeds her dog twice a day.
ex.ko: 그녀는 하루에 두 번 개에게 먹이를 준다.

ex.en: They store the feed in a big barn.

feel.en: experience a particular emotion or physical state

ex.en: I feel happy today.

feel.en: touch something to find out what it is like

ex.en: Feel this blanket, it's so soft.
ex.ko: 이 담요 만져 봐, 정말 부드러워.

feeling.en: an emotion or state of mind

ex.en: I have a strange feeling about this.
ex.ko: 나는 이것에 대해 이상한 느낌이 든다.

feeling.en: an idea or belief that is not based on clear facts

ex.en: I have a feeling she will call today.
ex.ko: 오늘 그녀가 전화할 것 같은 예감이 든다.

festival.en: a day or event when people celebrate something together

ex.en: The town holds a music festival every summer.
ex.ko: 그 마을은 매년 여름 음악 축제를 연다.

fever.en: a body temperature that is higher than normal, caused by illness

ex.en: He stayed home because he had a fever.

field.en: a piece of open land, often used for crops or animals

ex.en: Cows were grazing in the field.

field.en: an area of ground used for playing a sport

ex.en: The players ran onto the field.

field.en: a particular area of study or work

ex.en: She is an expert in the field of medicine.
She turned fifteen last week.
There were fifty people at the party.

fight.en: use physical force or arguments against someone

ex.en: The two boys fought over a toy.

fight.en: an act of fighting or arguing

ex.en: There was a big fight in the schoolyard.

file.en: a collection of documents or computer data kept together

ex.en: Please save the file on your computer.

file.en: officially submit a document or request

ex.en: She filed the report before the deadline.

fill.en: make something full, or become full

ex.en: He filled the glass with water.

find.en: discover something or someone, often after searching

ex.en: I can't find my keys.

find.en: come to think or feel something is true, based on experience

ex.en: I find this book really interesting.
ex.ko: 나는 이 책이 정말 흥미롭다고 생각한다.

ex.en: "How are you?" "I'm fine, thanks."

fine.en: an amount of money that must be paid as a punishment

ex.en: He had to pay a fine for parking illegally.
ex.ko: 그는 불법 주차로 벌금을 내야 했다.

fine.en: make someone pay money as a punishment

ex.en: She was fined for speeding.
The sand here is very fine.

finish.en: complete something or bring it to an end

ex.en: I need to finish my homework tonight.
ex.ko: 나는 오늘 밤 숙제를 끝내야 한다.

finish.en: the end point of a race or activity

ex.en: She was the first to cross the finish.

fire.en: the flame, heat, and light produced when something burns

ex.en: They sat around the fire and talked.
ex.ko: 그들은 불 주위에 둘러앉아 이야기를 나눴다.

ex.en: He was fired for being late every day.

fire.en: shoot a gun or other weapon

ex.en: The soldier fired at the target.

first.en: coming before all others in order or time

ex.en: This is my first visit to Korea.
First, wash your hands.

fish.en: an animal that lives in water and breathes through gills

ex.en: There are many colorful fish in the tank.
We went fishing at the lake.

fishing.en: the activity of trying to catch fish

ex.en: Fishing is his favorite hobby.
She has five brothers.
ex.ko: 그녀는 남자 형제가 다섯 명 있다.

flag.en: a piece of cloth with a design that represents a country or group

ex.en: The flag waved in the wind.

flat.en: having a level, smooth surface with no slope

ex.en: The land here is very flat.

flat.en: (British English) a set of rooms for living in, on one floor of a building

ex.en: They rent a small flat in London.
ex.ko: 그들은 런던에서 작은 아파트를 세내어 살고 있다.

floor.en: the flat surface of a room that you walk on

ex.en: The toy fell on the floor.
Our office is on the third floor.

flower.en: the colorful part of a plant that often has a pleasant smell

ex.en: She gave him a bunch of flowers.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그에게 꽃 한 다발을 주었다.

ex.en: Birds fly south for the winter.
A fly landed on the food.
ex.ko: 파리 한 마리가 음식 위에 앉았다.

fly.en: operate an aircraft, or travel by aircraft

ex.en: We flew to Japan last summer.
ex.ko: 우리는 지난여름 일본으로 비행기를 타고 갔다.

focus.en: give all your attention to one thing

ex.en: Please focus on your studies.

focus.en: the main thing that people's attention is directed at

ex.en: The focus of the meeting was next year's budget.

foggy.en: full of fog, making it hard to see far

ex.en: It was too foggy to drive fast.
ex.ko: 안개가 너무 짙어서 빨리 운전할 수 없었다.

ex.en: We left on the following morning.

following.en: a group of people who support or admire someone

ex.en: The singer has a huge following online.
ex.ko: 그 가수는 온라인에서 엄청난 팬층을 가지고 있다.

food.en: things that people or animals eat

ex.en: Korean food is very popular these days.

football.en: (British English) a game where two teams try to kick a ball into a goal

ex.en: He plays football every weekend.

football.en: (American English) a game played with an oval ball, popular in the US

ex.en: American football is very popular in the US.

for.en: used to show the purpose or person something is intended for

ex.en: This gift is for you.

for.en: used to show how long something lasts

ex.en: I waited for two hours.

foreign.en: belonging to or coming from a country that is not your own

ex.en: She is learning a foreign language.

foreigner.en: a person from a different country

ex.en: Many foreigners visit Seoul every year.
Don't forget to bring your umbrella.

form.en: a document with blank spaces for you to write information

ex.en: Please fill out this form.

form.en: the shape or structure of something

ex.en: The clouds took the form of animals.
They formed a new club at school.
My father is forty years old.
There are four seasons in a year.
She will be fourteen next month.
ex.ko: 그녀는 다음 달에 열네 살이 된다.

ex.en: The museum is free on Sundays.

free.en: able to do what you want, without being controlled

ex.en: Birds are free to fly wherever they want.
ex.ko: 새들은 어디든 자유롭게 날아갈 수 있다.

free.en: let a person or animal go, releasing them from being trapped

ex.en: They freed the bird from the cage.

friday.en: the day of the week after Thursday and before Saturday

ex.en: We usually go out on Friday nights.

friend.en: a person you know well and like

ex.en: She is my best friend.

frog.en: a small green animal that lives near water and jumps

ex.en: The frog jumped into the pond.

from.en: used to show the starting point or origin of something

ex.en: I got a letter from my friend.

front.en: the part of something that faces forward

ex.en: Let's meet in front of the station.

front.en: located at the front of something

ex.en: Please use the front door.

fruit.en: the sweet part of a plant that you can eat, such as an apple or banana

ex.en: I eat fruit every morning.

full.en: holding as much as possible; with no empty space left

ex.en: The bus was full, so we had to stand.
ex.ko: 버스가 만원이어서 우리는 서 있어야 했다.

ex.en: That game was really fun.
We had a lot of fun at the beach.
ex.ko: 우리는 해변에서 아주 즐거운 시간을 보냈다.

ex.en: He told a really funny joke.
There's a funny smell in the kitchen.

future.en: the time that is going to come

ex.en: No one knows what will happen in the future.
ex.ko: 미래에 무슨 일이 일어날지 아무도 모른다.

future.en: relating to a time that has not yet come

ex.en: She has big plans for her future career.
ex.ko: 그녀는 미래의 직업에 대해 큰 계획을 갖고 있다.

game.en: an activity or sport played by following rules

ex.en: Let's play a card game.

garbage.en: things that are thrown away because they are no longer wanted

ex.en: Take the garbage out, please.

garden.en: an area of land, often near a house, where flowers or vegetables are grown

ex.en: She grows tomatoes in her garden.

ghost.en: the spirit of a dead person that some people believe can appear

ex.en: The children told ghost stories at the campfire.
ex.ko: 아이들은 모닥불 앞에서 유령 이야기를 했다.

gift.en: something given to someone, especially on a special occasion

ex.en: Thank you for the gift.

girl.en: a female child or young woman

ex.en: The girl is playing with her dolls.
ex.ko: 그 소녀는 인형을 가지고 놀고 있다.

girlfriend.en: a girl or woman that someone is romantically involved with

ex.en: He introduced his girlfriend to his parents.

give.en: hand something to someone, or let them have it

ex.en: Can you give me a glass of water?
ex.ko: 물 한 잔 줄 수 있어?

ex.en: I'm so glad you came to visit.
ex.ko: 네가 놀러 와 줘서 정말 반가워.

glass.en: a hard, clear material used for windows and bottles

ex.en: Be careful, the glass can break easily.
ex.ko: 조심해, 유리는 쉽게 깨질 수 있어.

glass.en: a container made of glass used for drinking

ex.en: She poured a glass of milk.

glasses.en: two lenses in a frame worn over the eyes to help someone see better

ex.en: He wears glasses to read.
ex.ko: 그는 책을 읽을 때 안경을 쓴다.

go.en: move or travel from one place to another

ex.en: We go to school by bus.

go.cue: 한 번 해 보는 시도나 차례
go.en: an attempt or a turn at doing something

ex.en: Let me have a go at it.

goal.en: something that you hope to achieve

ex.en: Her goal is to become a doctor.

goal.cue: 축구 등에서 공을 넣어 얻는 점수
goal.en: a point scored by getting the ball into the goal area

ex.en: He scored the winning goal.

gold.en: a valuable yellow metal used to make jewelry and coins

ex.en: She wore a necklace made of gold.

gold.en: having the bright yellow color of gold

ex.en: The trophy was gold and shiny.
This is a good restaurant.
She did it for the good of the team.
ex.ko: 그녀는 팀의 이익을 위해 그렇게 했다.

good afternoon.en: a greeting used in the afternoon

ex.en: Good afternoon, everyone.

good morning.en: a greeting used in the morning

ex.en: Good morning! Did you sleep well?

good night.en: a phrase used when leaving someone at night or before sleeping

ex.en: Good night, sleep well.

grade.en: a level or year of school

ex.en: My son is in the third grade.

grade.en: a mark that shows how well someone did on a test or assignment

ex.en: She got a good grade on her math test.
ex.ko: 그녀는 수학 시험에서 좋은 성적을 받았다.

grade.en: decide the grade or score for something

ex.en: The teacher is grading our essays.

grammar.en: the rules for how words are used to make sentences

ex.en: English grammar can be tricky at first.
ex.ko: 영어 문법은 처음엔 까다로울 수 있다.

grandfather.en: the father of your father or mother

ex.en: My grandfather tells great stories.
ex.ko: 우리 할아버지는 이야기를 아주 재미있게 하신다.

ex.en: We visit Grandma every weekend.

grandmother.en: the mother of your father or mother

ex.en: My grandmother makes delicious kimchi.
ex.ko: 우리 할머니는 김치를 아주 맛있게 만드신다.

ex.en: Grandpa took us fishing.
She lives with her grandparents.

grape.cue: 작고 둥근 알이 송이로 열리는 과일
grape.en: a small, round, juicy fruit that grows in bunches

ex.en: She bought a bunch of grapes.

grass.en: a green plant that covers the ground

ex.en: The children played on the grass.

gray.en: a color between black and white

ex.en: She painted the wall gray.

gray.en: having the color between black and white

ex.en: He wore a gray suit.
That was a great movie.

green.en: the color of grass and leaves

ex.en: Green is her favorite color.

green.en: having the color of grass and leaves

ex.en: She wore a green dress.

greet.en: say hello to someone when you meet them

ex.en: She greeted us with a smile.

ground.en: the surface of the earth that you stand on

ex.en: The apple fell to the ground.

ground.en: a reason for a belief or action

ex.en: She had good grounds for her complaint.
ex.ko: 그녀는 불만을 제기할 정당한 근거가 있었다.

group.en: a number of people or things together

ex.en: We worked in small groups.

group.en: put people or things into groups

ex.en: The teacher grouped the students by age.
Children grow so fast.

grow.en: cause plants to develop by planting and caring for them

ex.en: We grow tomatoes in our backyard.

guess.en: give an answer without being sure it is correct

ex.en: Can you guess how old I am?

guess.en: an answer or opinion based on incomplete information

ex.en: It's just a guess, but I think she's right.
ex.ko: 그냥 추측이지만, 그녀가 맞는 것 같아.

guest.en: a person who is invited to visit or stay somewhere

ex.en: We are expecting guests for dinner.
ex.ko: 우리는 저녁 식사에 손님을 기다리고 있다.

guitar.en: a musical instrument with strings that you play with your fingers

ex.en: He plays the guitar in a band.

guy.en: an informal word for a man

ex.en: He seems like a nice guy.

habit.en: something you do often and regularly, almost without thinking

ex.en: Reading before bed is a good habit.
ex.ko: 자기 전에 책을 읽는 것은 좋은 습관이다.

ex.en: I had a headache yesterday.

hair.en: the thin strands that grow on your head

ex.en: She has long black hair.
ex.ko: 그녀는 길고 검은 머리카락을 가지고 있다.

haircut.en: an act of cutting someone's hair, or the resulting style

ex.en: He got a haircut before the interview.

half.cue: 둘로 똑같이 나눈 것 중 하나
half.en: one of two equal parts of something

ex.en: Can I have half of your sandwich?
ex.ko: 네 샌드위치 반 나눠 줄 수 있어?

ex.en: The cup is half full.

hall.en: a passage inside a building that connects rooms

ex.en: Lockers line the hall at school.

hall.en: a large room used for meetings or events

ex.en: The concert was held in a huge hall.

hamburger.cue: 빵 사이에 고기 패티를 넣은 음식
hamburger.en: a sandwich with a round piece of cooked meat inside a bun

ex.en: We had hamburgers for lunch.

hand.en: the part of your body at the end of your arm

ex.en: She raised her hand to ask a question.

hand.en: give something to someone by putting it in their hand

ex.en: Please hand me the salt.

handsome.en: (usually of a man) attractive in appearance

ex.en: He is a very handsome man.
What happened at the meeting?

happy.en: feeling or showing pleasure and satisfaction

ex.en: She looked very happy at the party.

has.en: the form of "have" used with he, she, or it

ex.en: She has two cats.
ex.ko: 그녀는 고양이를 두 마리 가지고 있다.

hat.en: something you wear on your head

ex.en: He wore a hat to block the sun.
I have two brothers.
ex.ko: 나는 남자 형제가 두 명 있다.

ex.en: Let's have lunch together.

have to.en: used to say that something is necessary

ex.en: I have to finish this by tomorrow.

he.en: used to refer to a man or boy already mentioned

ex.en: He is my brother.

head.en: the part of the body that contains the brain, eyes, and mouth

ex.en: She has a nice hat on her head.
ex.ko: 그녀는 머리에 예쁜 모자를 쓰고 있다.

ex.en: We headed home after the game.
ex.ko: 우리는 경기가 끝난 후 집으로 향했다.

ex.en: I have a terrible headache.

health.en: the condition of a person's body and mind

ex.en: Exercise is good for your health.
He looks healthy and strong.

hear.en: notice a sound with your ears

ex.en: Did you hear that noise?

heart.en: the organ in your chest that pumps blood

ex.en: Her heart was beating fast.

heart.en: the center of a person's feelings and emotions

ex.en: He has a kind heart.

heavy.en: having a lot of weight; not light

ex.en: This box is too heavy to carry.
ex.ko: 이 상자는 너무 무거워서 들 수 없다.

hello.en: a word used to greet someone

ex.en: Hello, how are you?

help.en: do something useful for someone who needs it

ex.en: Can you help me with my bags?
Thanks for your help.

her.en: used to refer to a woman already mentioned, or to show she owns something

ex.en: I saw her at the store.
Come here, please.

hers.en: the thing or things belonging to a woman already mentioned

ex.en: This umbrella is hers.

hi.en: an informal way to greet someone

ex.en: Hi! Nice to meet you.

hide.en: go somewhere so that you cannot be seen

ex.en: The cat hid under the bed.

hide.en: put something where others cannot find it

ex.en: She hid the present in the closet.
That is a very high mountain.

high.en: at or to a great height

ex.en: The bird flew high in the sky.

hill.en: an area of land that is higher than the land around it, but lower than a mountain

ex.en: They walked up the hill.

him.en: used to refer to a man or boy already mentioned, as the object of a verb

ex.en: I called him yesterday.

his.en: belonging to a man or boy already mentioned

ex.en: This is his book.

history.en: the study of events that happened in the past

ex.en: She loves reading about history.
ex.ko: 그녀는 역사에 대해 읽는 것을 좋아한다.

hobby.en: an activity that you do for pleasure in your free time

ex.en: My hobby is playing the guitar.

hole.en: an opening or empty space in something

ex.en: There is a hole in my sock.

holiday.en: a day or period when you do not have to work or go to school

ex.en: We are going to the beach for the holidays.
ex.ko: 우리는 휴가 동안 해변에 갈 것이다.

ex.en: I stayed home all day.

home.en: to or at the place where you live

ex.en: Let's go home now.

hometown.en: the town or city where you were born or grew up

ex.en: I miss my hometown a lot.

homework.en: school work that students do at home

ex.en: I still have to finish my homework.

hope.en: want something to happen and believe it might

ex.en: I hope you feel better soon.

hope.en: a feeling that something good may happen

ex.en: She never lost hope.

horse.en: a large animal that people ride or use to pull things

ex.en: He rides a horse every weekend.

hospital.en: a place where sick or injured people are treated

ex.en: She was taken to the hospital.
Be careful, the soup is hot.

hotel.en: a building where travelers pay to stay for a night or more

ex.en: We booked a hotel near the beach.

hour.en: a unit of time equal to 60 minutes

ex.en: The movie is two hours long.
They bought a new house last year.

house.en: provide space for something or someone

ex.en: The museum houses many famous paintings.
ex.ko: 그 박물관은 유명한 그림을 많이 소장하고 있다.

how.en: used to ask about the way something happens or is done

ex.en: How do you make this dish?
I'm so hungry, let's eat now.

hurry.en: do something quickly because there is little time

ex.en: Hurry up, we're going to be late.

hurry.en: the need to do something quickly

ex.en: There's no hurry, take your time.

hurt.en: cause pain or injury to someone

ex.en: I fell and hurt my knee.
Are you hurt?
